Ïù∏Î≤§ÌÜÝÎ¶¨ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖúÏûêÎèôÎ≥ëÌï©‚îÉInventory Slots Auto-Sorting
GameSetting/AutomaticInventoryItemRestock‚îÉWhen there are multiple consumables of the same type in different inventory slots, items will be consumed starting from the lowest priority slot.\nRegardless of the hotkey used, slots positioned further to the bottom right are considered lower priority.\nExample: If there's Steak in Slots 1, 3, and 5, pressing any hotkey for these slots will consume the Steak from slot 5 first.
ÏòµÏÖò/VLSÏÉÅÌò∏ÏûëÏö©ÌôïÏû•ÎßµÌôúÏÑ±Ìôî‚îÉEnable Expanded Map for VLS

Ïù∏Î≤§ÌÜÝÎ¶¨ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖúÏö∞ÏÑÝÏÇ¨Ïö©ÏÑ§ÏÝï‚îÉItem Consumption Priority Settings
GameSetting/InventoryItemUsePriority‚îÉWhen there are multiple consumables of the same type in different inventory slots, they will be consumed according to the designated settings, regardless of the assigned hotkey.\nThe top-left inventory slot is Slot 1, and the bottom-right one is Slot 10.\nExample:\nAscending:  If there's Steak in Slots 1, 3, and 5, pressing any hotkey for these slots will consume the Steak from slot 1 first.\nDescending: If there's Steak in Slots 1, 3, and 5, pressing any hotkey for these slots will consume the Steak from Slot 5 first.

SeasonCollection/Info/Desc‚îÉWhen you use a Season Fragments Bundle obtained from Daily Missions, the Store, and other sources, you'll receive Season Fragments and added to your Season Collection.\nCollect all Season Fragments to complete the collection and claim your reward!
SeasonCollection/Tooltip/Unregistered‚îÉUse Exchange Fragments to get Season Fragments.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Title‚îÉSeason Collection Rewards
SeasonCollection/Reward/Grade‚îÉLevel {0} Reward
SeasonCollection/Reward/Condition‚îÉAvailable after claiming reward from previous level
SeasonCollection/Point/Title‚îÉSeason Fragment Points
SeasonCollection/Point/Info/1‚îÉ*You can spend Exchange Fragments to use Season Fragments.
SeasonCollection/Point/Info/2‚îÉ*If you obtain a Season Fragment that you've already used, it will be converted into a certain amount of Season Fragment Points based on its rarity.(Uncommon:10Pt / Common:1Pt)
SeasonCollection/Point/Info/3‚îÉ*At the end of the season, any unused Exchange Fragments and Season Fragment Points will be reset.
SeasonCollection/Popup/Confirm/Title‚îÉUse Season Fragments
SeasonCollection/Popup/Confirm‚îÉSpend 1 Exchange Fragment to use the selected Season Fragment?
SeasonCollection/Popup/Reject‚îÉNot enough Exchange Fragments.

SeasonCollection/Popup/NotYetProgress‚îÉThis level is not available yet. Check your current Season Collection level in progress.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Title/1‚îÉSeason Collection
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/1/1‚îÉ*Collect Season Fragments to complete the Season Collection and get rewards.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/1/2‚îÉ*You must complete the current level of the Season Collection to unlock a new one.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/1/3‚îÉ*Fragments collected in your current level are not accumulated when they're duplicated, instead they're converted into Exchange Points.
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Title/2‚îÉHow to Get Season Fragments
SeasonCollection/Reward/Tooltip/Info/2‚îÉ*You can get Season Fragments through different means such as gameplay rewards, daily missions, login events, personal store, daily boxes, etc.

CharacterGuide/1/OneHandSword‚îÉ<color=orange>Daggers</color> are powerful for instant attacks. You can disappear for a moment and reappear again with the Cloak and Dagger skill to deal cellular damage.
CharacterGuide/1/TwoHandSword‚îÉ<color=orange>Two-handed Swords</color> are weaker than other weapons in the first half of the match but get really powerful in the second half. The weapon skill Parry can block the enemy's main attack once, so it becomes even scarier in the second half.
CharacterGuide/1/Axe‚îÉ<color=orange>Axes</color> are stronger in the earlier stages of the game. The weapon skill, Maleficent Helix, allows you to deal extra damage and heal after getting stacks from basic attacks.
CharacterGuide/1/DualSword‚îÉ<color=orange>Dual Swords</color> are kind of hidden weapons. You cannot choose them from the start - you have to make twin swords first, using a kitchen knife and a longsword. The specialty of having two swords is being able to perform fast attacks, and although their attack power is low, they are most effective when paired with the Dual Sword Rampage weapon skill and Adrenaline Burst (W), which maximize the life steal effect.

CharacterGuide/2/SniperRifle‚îÉ<color=orange>Sniper</color>s require good use of telephoto cameras and traps in order to take advantage of the vision range and long distance of the Sniper Rifles. If Aya succeeds in predicting her enemy's movements, she can hit them at a very long distance with the Sniper skill, Ballistic Advantage.
CharacterGuide/2/AssaultRifle‚îÉ<color=orange>Assault Rifles</color> most important feature is that their basic attack fires 3 round bursts. Your movement will be restricted for the 3 round bursts. Remember to activate the weapon skill for even MORE damage!

The weapon skill Shadow Stab has a knockback effect, in addition to movement speed reduction, so you can maintain distance when fighting.
CharacterGuide/4/Hammer‚îÉ<color=orange>Hammers</color> are weapons with strong attack power. The Armor Piercing weapon skill has a defense power reduction effect, so you can quickly suppress enemies with low defense power.
CharacterGuide/4/Bat‚îÉ<color=orange>Bats</color> have an advantage by being faster. By using the Full Swing skill, you can stun the enemy - by using it with Heavy Strike (E), Magnus can become very scary when fighting against an enemy between four walls.

CharacterGuide/5/DirectFire‚îÉ<color=orange>Shuriken</color> have tricky recipes for high tier weapons compared to throwing weapons. The weapon skill, Caltrops, stays on the ground for a long time, allowing for more favorable fights by positioning yourself based on where your opponent is coming from or where you're headed.

ÎÇòÎ≠áÍ∞ÄÏßÄ ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉYou can collect a <color=white>Branch</color> from a woodpile in every area. \n\nBranches may seem worthless (after all, it's just a little stick) but they're essential components in a lot of items!

ÍøÄÌÉÑ Ïö∞ÏúÝ ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉYou can make <color=#35FF94>Honey Milk</color> by combining... <color=white>Milk</color> and <color=white>Honey</color>!\n\nFind <color=white>Milk</color> and <color=white>Honey</color> in boxes around Lumia Island and try making <color=#35FF94>Honey Milk</color>.
ÎßàÎäòÎπµ ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉCombine <color=white>Garlic</color> with <color=white>Bread</color> to make <color=#35FF94>Garlic Bread</color>. Íµ¨Ïö¥ ÎßàÎäò ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉYou can cook <color=#2AC0FF>Roasted Garlic</color> with <color=white>Garlic</color>.\n\nIf you find <color=white>Garlic</color> in a box on Lumia Island, go to a <color=white>Campfire</color> to make <color=#2AC0FF>Roasted Garlic</color>.
Í∞êÏûê ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉCollect <color=white>Potatoes</color>. They mainly grow in the northern part of Lumia Island.\n\nTake the <color=white>Potatoes</color> you collected, cook them over a Campfire, and get even better food.
ÎåÄÍµ¨ ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉIf you fish from a waterside location, you can catch <color=white>Cod</color>.\n\nTake the <color=white>Cod</color> you caught to a Campfire and make even better food.
Ïö¥ÏÑù ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉMeteorites impact once a day using meteorite guidance technology.\n\nA Meteorite will impact from Day 2 to Day 4 in a random area. The impact location is announced a minute before it occurs.\n\nAfter the <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color> impacts, you can collect it - stay nearby if you need it!
ÏÉùÎ™ÖÏùò ÎÇòÎ¨¥ ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉSome trees have accumulated VF (Vital Force) over time. You can collect <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color>, also known as the World Tree, and harness its power!\n\nA Tree of Life spawns on Day 2 and Day 3, and an announcement occurs 1 minute before it spawns.\n
VFÌòàÏï°ÏÉòÌîå ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉVF (Vital Force) is theorized to be the cause of physically impossible phenomena.\n\nHumans "infused" with VF have been found to have various abilities and powerful regenerative abilities. Experiments testing this were conducted on Lumia Island. \n\nWickeline drops a <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Sample</color> when slain. She appears on Night 4 and roams around Lumia Island.
Ìè¨Ïä§ÏΩîÏñ¥ ÏïÑÏù¥ÌÖú ÌöçÎìù‚îÉ<color=#D05EFF>Force Cores</color> are from a natural ore enriched with VF. It can be used to replicate items and weapons from legends and myths.\n\nCombine <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color> and <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> to make a <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>, or get it from Omega.\n\n
ÌîåÎÝàÏù¥Ïñ¥/ÏïºÏÉùÎèôÎ¨º ÌîºÌï¥ Ï£ºÍ∏∞‚îÉBefore you start fighting, get stronger first.\n\nPrioritize building your weapon, and then attack your enemies and build your weapon mastery!\n
Îã≠ Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉChickens are the weakest Wild Animal. \nSlaying chickens will drop <color=white>Leather</color>, and if you're lucky, <color=white>Meat</color> and <color=white>Feathers</color>.\n\nSlay Chickens to obtain materials and use them to craft equipment and food.
Î∞ïÏ•ê Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉBats spawn 30 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Bat will drop a <color=white>Telephoto Camera</color> and <color=white>Leather</color>, which will give you vision for a limited time.\n\nSlayed Bats will reappear in the same spot after 160 seconds.
Î©ßÎèºÏßÄ Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉBoars appear 60 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Boar will drop two chunks of <color=white>Meat</color> and <color=white>Leather</color>, but be careful of the boar's charge attack!\n\n\nSlayed Boars will reappear in the same spot after 150 seconds.
Îì§Í∞ú Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉWild Dogs appear 30 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Wild Dog will drop <color=white>Leather</color> and a <color=white>Common Material Item</color>, but beware that their first attack will reduce your healing!\n\nSlayed Wild Dogs will reappear in the same spot after 160 seconds.
ÎäëÎåÄ Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉWolves appear 90 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Wolf will drop <color=white>Meat</color>, <color=white>Leather</color>, and one <color=#35FF94>Uncommon Material Item</color>.\n\nWolves of Level 10 or above will sometimes drop <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> or <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>.\nBe careful‚ÄîWolves like to roam in groups of two, and they can call their friends to joint attack!\n\nSlayed Wolves will reappear in the same spot after 190 seconds.
Í≥∞ Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉBears appear 150 seconds after the start of the experiment.\nSlaying a Bear will drop <color=white>Meat</color>, <color=white>Leather</color>, and one <color=#35FF94>Uncommon Material Item</color>.\n\nBears of Level 7 and above will sometimes drop <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color>, and <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>.\nBe careful as the bear will stun you if you take a hit.\n\n\nSlayed Bears will reappear in the same spot after 250 seconds.
ÏúÑÌÅ¥ÎùºÏù∏ Î∞ïÏÇ¨ Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉWickeline is believed to have been involved in earlier experiments in some way, but nobody is still around to confirm it.\n\nShe comes out from the Research Center and starts to move around Lumia Island at the beginning of Night 4.\n\nShe is tough, but drops some goodies: a <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Sample</color> and additional item rewards! \n\nKill her and you'll also get an "Enhanced by Vital Force" buff for 3 minutes.\n\nSubjects Enhanced by Vital Force cause extra damage over 3 seconds when they attack. \n\nWickeline is strong, but is worth the risk to take down.
ÏÇ¨ÎÉ• ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉHunt Wild Animals to increase your Hunt Mastery.\n\nYou can also increase Hunt Mastery when dealing damage to or taking damage from Wild Animals.\n\nYou will deal more damage to Wild Animals as your Hunt Mastery goes up.
ÏÝúÏûë ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉCraft items to increase Craft Mastery.\n\nYou can increase Craft Mastery when crafting Weapons, Head Armor, Chest Armor, Arm/Accesory Armor, Leg Armor, and Food.\n\nOut of combat HP Regen will increase as your Crafting Mastery goes up.
ÌÉêÏÉâ ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉExplore Lumia Island to increase your Search Mastery.\n\nYou can increase Search Mastery by opening item boxes, opening air supplies, collecting resources, and operating security consoles.
Ïù¥Îèô ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉMove around the island to increase your Movement Mastery.\n\nTraveling far, moving to new areas, and crafting Leg Armor all also increase your mastery.\n\nYour Movement Speed will increase as your Movement Mastery goes up.
Ï≤¥ÎÝ• ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉIncrease your Health Mastery!\n\nYour Health Mastery is increased when you make food or take damage.
Î∞©Ïñ¥ ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉIncrease your Defense Mastery by taking damage! Just don't die. \n\nYou can increase your Defense Mastery by crafting Head Armor and taking damage from players, wild animals, or traps.\n\nWhen your Defense Mastery goes up, you take less damage.
Î™ÖÏÉÅ ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉIncrease your Meditation Mastery!\n\nYou can increase your Meditation Mastery by using SP or crafting drinks.\n\nWhen your Meditation Mastery goes up, so does your SP.
Ìï®ÏÝï ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉIncrease your Trap Mastery by actively using traps.\n\nYou can increase your Trap Mastery by crafting traps, setting traps, or dealing damage with traps.\n\nThe higher your Trap Mastery, the more damage your traps do.
Î¨¥Í∏∞ ÏàôÎÝ®ÎèÑ Îã¨ÏÑ±ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉIncrease your Weapon Mastery by attacking enemies.\n\nIncrease your Weapon Mastery by dealing damage to or killing players and Wild Animals, or by crafting weapons.\n\nYour Basic Attack and Skill Damage will increase as your Weapon Mastery goes up.
ÏïåÌåå Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉAlpha is an Android powered by VF (Vital Force).\n\nAlpha appears on Night 2 in two random areas.\n\nAlpha drops <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color> and <color=#35FF94>Recon Drone</color> when it is killed.
Ïò§Î©îÍ∞Ä Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉOmega is an Android that is powered by VF (Vital Force).\n\nOmega appears on Night 3. It will not spawn in the same area as Alpha.\n\nOmega uses special abilities from its VF, but often becomes unstable, revealing its weakness whenever it takes over a certain amount of damage.\n\nOmega drops <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color> and <color=#2AC0FF>EMP Drone</color> when it is killed.
Î£®ÎØ∏ÏïÑ ÌÅ¨ÎÝàÎîß ÌöçÎìùÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉThe <color=#F4FF63>Credit</color> is a currency used on Lumia Island as part of an experiment.\n\nPlayers start the game with some Credits. Throughout the game, players earn Credits upon each day/night change, killing an enemy test subject, or slaying Wild or Mutant Animals.\n\n<color=#F4FF63>Credits</color> are used to call in Remote Drones or use Kiosks.
Î£®ÎØ∏ÏïÑ ÏÝÑÏÜ°ÎìúÎ°Ý ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉRemote Drones were originally stealth surveillance drones used to track test subjects on Lumia Island.\n\nSince then, researchers have given these drones the ability to deliver materials to test subjects who pay for it with <color=#F4FF63>Credits</color>!\n\nNot having good box RNG? Is your required material only left in restricted areas? Leave it to a Transfer Drone!
Î£®ÎØ∏ÏïÑ ÏÝÑÏÜ°ÏΩòÏÜî ÏÇ¨Ïö©ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉResearchers have placed Kiosks across Lumia Island to intensify the results of their experiments.\nAt a Kiosk, test subjects can pay <color=#F4FF63>Credits</color> to call in a Transfer Supply Box.\n
ÏΩîÎ∞úÌä∏ Î™®Îìú ÏäπÎ¶¨ÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉTry out Cobalt Protocol, the second mode in Eternal Return!\n\nIf you need a break from crafting and farming, take on the fast-paced combat of Cobalt Protocol! Also, you won't have to worry about placement.\nAchieve victory by depleting the enemy team's Life Score!
ÏΩîÎ∞úÌä∏ Î™®Îìú Ïã§ÌóòÏ≤¥ Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉRush into combat in Cobalt Protocol without the risk of being eliminated from the game!

ÏΩîÎ∞úÌä∏ Î™®Îìú Ï£ΩÏßÄ ÏïäÍ≥Ý Ïã§ÌóòÏ≤¥ Ïó∞ÏÜç Ï≤òÏπòÌïòÍ∏∞‚îÉAchieve a killstreak by killing multiple test subjects in a row without dying!\n\nAs you kill more test subjects without dying, you'll gain reputation. A high reputation lets you earn more Credits!

Every user is entitled to the following:\n ‚Äì The right to access: You have the right to request Nimble Neuron for copies of your personal data. You also have the right to request Nimble Neuron to complete the information you believe is incomplete.\n ‚Äì The right to erasure: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron erase your personal data, under certain conditions.\n ‚Äì The right to restrict processing: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron restrict the processing of your personal data, under certain conditions.\n ‚Äì The right to object to processing: You have the right to object to Nimble Neuron‚Äôs processing of your personal data, under certain conditions.\n ‚Äì The right to data portability: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron transfer the data that we have collected to another organization, or directly to you, under certain conditions.\n If you make a request, we have one month to respond to you. In some cases, however, particularly if your request is more complex, more time may be required up to a maximum of three months after the data on which we received the request. In this case you will, of course, be kept fully informed of our progress. If you would like to exercise any of these rights, please contact us at our email: eternalreturn@nimbleneuron.com.\n\n8. COOKIES\n8.1 Cookies are text files placed on your computer to collect standard Internet log information and visitor behavior information. When you visit our websites, we may collect information from you automatically through cookies or similar technology. For further information, visit allaboutcookies.org.\n8.2 Nimble Neuron uses cookies in a range of ways to improve your experience on our website, including:\n ‚Äì Keeping you signed in.\n ‚Äì Understanding how you use our website.\n ‚Äì Provide you with personalized preferences setting.\n8.3 There are a number of different types of cookies, however, our website uses:\n ‚Äì Functionality: Nimble Neuron may use these cookies so that we recognize you on our website and remember your previously selected preferences. These could include what language you prefer and location you are in. A mix of first-party and third-party cookies are used.\n ‚Äì Advertising: Nimble Neuron may use these cookies to collect information about your visit to our website, the content you viewed, the links you followed and information about your browser, device, and your IP address. Nimble Neuron may share limited aspects of this data with third parties for advertising purposes. Nimble Neuron may also share online data collected through cookies with our advertising partners. This means that when you visit another website, you may be shown advertising based on your browsing patterns on our website.\n8.4 You can set your browser not to accept cookies, and the above website tells you how to remove cookies from your browser. However, in a few cases, some of our website features may not function as a result.\n \n9. PRIVACY POLICIES OF OTHER WEBSITE\n9.1 The Nimble Neuron website(s) contains links to other websites. Our privacy policy applies only to our website, so if you click on a link to another website, you should read their privacy policy.\n\n10. CHANGES TO OUR PRIVACY POLICY\n10.1 Nimble Neuron keeps its privacy policy under regular review and places any updates on this web page. You will be deemed to have accepted such updates by continuing to use Nimble Neuron‚Äôs service. This privacy policy was last updated on January 22th, 2026.\n\n11. YOUR PRIVACY RIGHTS\n11.1 In certain regions, you have a right to report a complaint or if you have concerns about how we process your personal data. Please contact your local authority for more information.\n\n12. (‚ÄúNimble Neuron‚Äù) uses the personal data we collect from you when you use any of Nimble Neuron‚Äôs games (‚ÄúGame‚Äù).\n \n 1. DATA WE COLLECT\n 1.1 Nimble Neuron may collect the following data for your use of the Game. Failure to provide any one of the data listed below may result in your inability to use any one of Nimble Neuron‚Äôs Game:\n\n (i) Information directly from you: Your personal identification information (name, email address, phone number, date of birth, country of residence, etc.), in-Game identification information such as user ID, and nickname. Nimble Neuron uses Zendesk, Inc., for its customer service. Please visit Zendesk‚Äôs privacy policy at https://www.zendesk.com/company/customers-partners/privacy-policy, and GeekNation‚Äôs privacy policy at https://geeknation.info/privacy-policy/.\n (ii) Information collected automatically: IP address, internet service provider, country code, computer operating system, hardware information, application software, country, game language, chat log.\n (iii) Information collected from third parties: If the Game is distributed and payment is handled by Microsoft, its software distribution platform may collect your account information, along with payment information from you. Please review the Microsoft Terms of Sale for more information: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/storedocs/terms-of-sale. \n \n 2. HOW WE USE YOUR DATA\n 2.1 Nimble Neuron collects your data so that we can:\n - Identify your account and provide you with customer support.\n - Answer your questions that you inquired through our customer support. \n - Provide technical support such as bug fixes and patches, improve our Game service.\n - Provide technical protection against usage of unauthorized programs or improper gameplay that could harm Game users. \n - Track usage patterns and generate statistics, such as a leaderboard and usage, from service use. \n - Send or distribute a prize related to an event/promotion. This information shall be deleted once such event/promotion is complete and the prize(s) is received by the winner(s). \n \n 3. HOW WE STORE YOUR DATA\n 3.1 Generally, your data will be stored until purpose of collecting such data is achieved. However, Nimble Neuron may still store your data if it is required under applicable regulation, law, this privacy policy, or any other related agreement made between you and Nimble Neuron. Nimble Neuron securely stores your data in accordance with appropriate technical measures. However, due to unforeseen circumstances, Nimble Neuron does not guarantee loss, misuse, or alternation of your data by third parties.\n \n 4. INTERNATIONAL TRANSFER\n 4.1 Your personal data may be transferred outside the European Union, your countries, or regions that you are located. If your personal data is transferred, we will take all reasonable steps to protect any personal data are treated securely in accordance with this Privacy Policy and relying on our Game‚Äôs hosting service, Amazon Web Services, which it complies with the EU ‚Äì US Privacy Shield. To learn more about the Privacy Shield program please visit http://www.privacyshield.gov. \n \n 5. YOUR DATA PROTECTION RIGHTS\n 5.1 Nimble Neuron would like to make sure you are fully aware of all of your data protection rights. We may charge you a small fee for this service.\n - The right to rectification: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron correct any information you believe is inaccurate. You also have the right to request Nimble Neuron to complete the information you believe is incomplete.\n - The right to erasure: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron erase your personal data, under certain conditions.\n - The right to restrict processing: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron restrict the processing of your personal data, under certain conditions.\n - The right to object to processing: You have the right to object to Nimble Neuron‚Äôs processing of your personal data, under certain conditions.\n - The right to data portability: You have the right to request that Nimble Neuron transfer the data that we have collected to another organization, or directly to you, under certain conditions.\n If you make a request, we have one month to respond to you. If you would like to exercise any of these rights, please contact us at our email: eternalreturn@nimbleneuron.com. \n \n 6. When you visit our websites, we may collect information from you automatically through cookies or similar technology. For further information, visit allaboutcookies.org.\n \n 6.2 Nimble Neuron uses cookies in a range of ways to improve your experience on our website, including:\n - Keeping you signed in.\n - Understanding how you use our website.\n - Provide you with personalized preferences setting. \n \n 6.3 There are a number of different types of cookies, however, our website uses:\n - Functionality: Nimble Neuron may use these cookies so that we recognize you on our website and remember your previously selected preferences. These could include what language you prefer and location you are in. A mix of first-party and third-party cookies are used.\n - Advertising: Nimble Neuron may use these cookies to collect information about your visit to our website, the content you viewed, the links you followed and information about your browser, device, and your IP address. Nimble Neuron may share limited aspects of this data with third parties for advertising purposes. Nimble Neuron may also share online data collected through cookies with our advertising partners. When his <color=white>VF Gauge</color> is full he enters a <color=white>VF Overflow</color> state for a period of time and his increased damage is applied to the max. \n\n<color=white>VF Overflow:</color> Echion‚Äôs movement speed is increased and he periodically deals true damage to enemies nearby, activating <color=white>Envenomization(R)</color> and resetting the cooldown of  <color=white>Dry Bite(E)</color>.\nWhile VF Overflow is active, killing an enemy increases the duration of Overflow and resets the cooldown of  <color=white>Dry Bite(E)</color>.\n\n<color=white>Exuviation:</color> When Overflow ends, he enters Exuviation for a period of time, reducing his attack range and disabling <color=white>VF Gauge</color> increase.\n\n<color=white>Envenomization(R):</color> Echion fires unstable VF in a targeted direction, dealing skill damage. The duration of Overflow increases when he deals damage to enemy characters with <color=white>Envenomization</color>. Dealing damage with basic attacks reduces the cooldown of <color=white>Envenomization</color>.\n\n<i>Envenomization‚Äôs effects change based on currently used weapon.</i>\n<i>Fixed Cooldown: This skill is not affected by Cooldown Reduction.</i>

MasteryType/AssaultRifle/Desc‚îÉAssault rifles are long-ranged weapons that fire in bursts and specialize in rapid and constant attacks.  
MasteryType/SniperRifle/Desc‚îÉSniper rifles have high Attack Power and the longest attack range. They cover more distance than range of vision, so be sure to carefully select a position and secure a good range of vision.

WeaponType/Desc/Glove‚îÉGlove is a melee weapon useful for quick, consecutive attacks.
WeaponType/Desc/Tonfa‚îÉTonfa is a melee weapon with high viability.
WeaponType/Desc/Bat‚îÉBat is a melee weapon that can handle enemies with ease.
WeaponType/Desc/Whip‚îÉWhip is a melee weapon specialized in controlling ranged battles.
WeaponType/Desc/HighAngleFire‚îÉThrow is a ranged weapon that is perfect for blocking vision during long-range battles.
WeaponType/Desc/DirectFire‚îÉShuriken is a ranged weapon that deals heavy damage for both skills and basic attacks.
WeaponType/Desc/Bow‚îÉBow is a ranged weapon that has the potential for high growth.
WeaponType/Desc/CrossBow‚îÉCrossbow is a ranged weapon that holds approaching enemies back and controls ranged fighting.
WeaponType/Desc/Pistol‚îÉPistol is a ranged weapon that offers stylish/flashy combat through quick attack speed.
WeaponType/Desc/AssaultRifle‚îÉAssault Rifle is a ranged weapon that is proficient in continuous combat through fast attack speed.
WeaponType/Desc/SniperRifle‚îÉSniper Rifle is a ranged weapon best used for long ranges and strong single-shot attacks.
WeaponType/Desc/Hammer‚îÉHammer is a melee weapon that is slow to attack but offers powerful hits.
WeaponType/Desc/Axe‚îÉAxe is a melee weapon built to last in combat.
WeaponType/Desc/OneHandSword‚îÉDagger is a melee weapon best for surprise attacks and instant, close-range battle.
WeaponType/Desc/TwoHandSword‚îÉTwo-handed Sword is a melee weapon that is well-balanced in all stats.
WeaponType/Desc/DualSword‚îÉDuals Swords are melee weapons capable of stylish combat.
WeaponType/Desc/Spear‚îÉSpear is a melee weapon made for mid-range battle.
WeaponType/Desc/Nunchaku‚îÉNunchaku is a melee weapon that is versatile in different kinds of battle.
WeaponType/Desc/Rapier‚îÉRapier is a melee weapon that allows you to quickly rush your enemy.
WeaponType/Desc/Guitar‚îÉGuitar is a ranged weapon optimitzed to support your allies.
WeaponType/Desc/Camera‚îÉCamera is a ranged weapon that can block enemy vision range.
WeaponType/Desc/Arcana‚îÉArcana is a ranged weapon that uses a combination of magic to fight.
WeaponType/Desc/VFArm‚îÉVF Prosthetics is a melee weapon that can be upgraded without combining different weapons.

gameTip_Desc_10070_1‚îÉActivate the security console in each area of Lumia Island to gain important sight on the enemies' location. gameTip_Title_10071_1‚îÉHyperloop & VLS
gameTip_Desc_10071_1‚îÉBig changes are coming to the Hyperloop system.\nStarting on Day 3, the new VLS transportation devices will be available in Safe Zones, while Hyperloops will be dynamically activated in Restricted Areas.\nUnlike Hyperloops, the VLS allows movement by "gliding" within a certain radius.
gameTip_Title_10071_2‚îÉHyperloop
gameTip_Desc_10071_2‚îÉDid someone say, FAST TRAVEL?\n\nIt may look like a broken down phone booth, but this booth allows you to travel anywhere on Lumia Island thanks to Hyperloop technology. You can also tap on a <color=#8FDBF8>teammate's picture</color> to be teleported directly to them!\n\n*Hyperloop Usage Precautions*\nYou may accidentally run into enemies after teleporting. Be careful!
gameTip_Title_10071_3‚îÉVLS
gameTip_Desc_10071_3‚îÉUnlike Hyperloops, the VLS isn't designed for long-distance travel but enables synchronized gliding with allies.\nWhile you're gliding, the ground becomes visible below a certain altitude, making it excellent for scouting.\nLanding after gliding triggers a brief area-wide stun, making it useful for initiating.\nOnly the Host can control the gliding, with allies relying on the Host for synchronized movement. At a certain altitude, allies can break away from the Host and move freely.
gameTip_Title_10072_1‚îÉLaunch Pad
gameTip_Desc_10072_1‚îÉThe Launch Pad allows you to easily and quickly move to distant areas!\nThey can be crucial in confrontations with enemies, so practice using them strategically!
gameTip_Title_10090_1‚îÉQuick Communication
gameTip_Desc_10090_1‚îÉYou can ping anyone by clicking ALT + the left mouse button.\n\nEven if there's no time to chat, use the ping function to communicate in a variety of ways. Send an exclamation point to allies to alert them to "danger ahead!"\n\nIf you need a bit more in-depth communication, click CTRL and drag the left mouse button for the <color=#8FDBF8>quick chatting function</color>. Click CTRL + the left mouse button to <color=#8FDBF8>set goals with your team</color>.
gameTip_Title_10100_1‚îÉDetonation Timer
gameTip_Desc_10100_1‚îÉIf you're in a Restricted Area marked in red, you better get out of there, stat! If not, you'll die as soon as the detonation timer goes off.\n\nDetonation timers are placed in Restricted Areas and activate with terrifying effects. When the timer reaches 0, you can kiss your life goodbye.\n\nYou can restore the detonation timer by killing other test subjects, but it's better for you not to be there at all.\n\n"Soon the Test Subject will explode!"
gameTip_Title_10110_1‚îÉTemporary Safe Zone
gameTip_Desc_10110_1‚îÉWhen the last safe zone is designated as a Restricted Area, two temporary safe zones are created if <color=#8FDBF8>3 or more survivors</color> are still alive.\n\nThe detonation timer won't tick down if your team is inside the safe zone. On the other hand, if 2 or more teams are in the temporary safe zone, or if they leave the temporary safe zone, the detonation timer begins ticking again.\n\nIt's a piece of cake. Keep your team in the temporary safe zone, and drive out any enemies to stay alive!
gameTip_Title_10120_1‚îÉFinal Safe Zone
gameTip_Desc_10120_1‚îÉThe final safe zone is a place to determine the ultimate winner.\n\nThe final safe zone forms in a circular area around the Chronosphere located in the last restricted area. Outside of this safe zone, the explosion timer will activate.\n\nAfter a certain period of time, the safe zones will gradually begin to disappear. Eventually, all safe zones will disappear, and the explosion timer will activate across the entire map. This is your time to defeat the enemy and claim first place!
gameTip_Title_10130_1‚îÉAuto Revive
gameTip_Desc_10130_1‚îÉDied? Don't give up just yet!\n\nIf your teammates are still alive, you will auto-revive until Night 2. An enemy guarding your dead body? No problem - you can choose the spot to respawn. A place close to your team should be safe... right?
gameTip_Title_10140_1‚îÉDowned / Slain
gameTip_Desc_10140_1‚îÉTest subjects on Lumia Island become downed when they are first slain. When down, you can only move or discard items, and your detonation timer and HP drop even if you stand still.\n\nIf you die again while down, your dead body will be left behind. You can grab items left from dead test subjects. If you hunt a test subject that has a Legendary item, you can earn a large amount of Credits through bounty items.
gameTip_Title_10140_2‚îÉDowned Skill
gameTip_Desc_10140_2‚îÉDon't give up when you're downed!\nThere are some special skills that can only be used while downed!\n\nThere are a total of 3 Downed Skills:\n1. <color=#8FDBF8>Obstinacy</color>, which temporarily increases movement speed while downed. \n2. <color=#8FDBF8>Fission Blast</color>, which makes test subjects to self-destruct.\n3. <color=#8FDBF8>AED</color>, which allows test subjects to self-revive after a certain amount of time.\n\nCreate a more diverse gameplay by strategically using Downed Skills!

gameTip_Title_10270_2‚îÉEscape Conditions

gameTip_Title_10270_4‚îÉEscape Voting
gameTip_Desc_10270_4‚îÉYour team has to vote in order to escape.\nOnce the team meets the escape conditions and purchases a Loop Card Key - Red from a Kiosk, a vote is conducted.\n\nThere are two rounds of voting, each lasting 30 seconds. You can only escape if the majority of the team agrees to escape. If you don't participate, your vote counts as in favor.\n\nThe second round of voting is automatically carried a certain amount of time later.
gameTip_Title_10270_5‚îÉHow to Escape

Life Points will be immediately deducted depending on whether they were captured or not.

gameTip_Title_10160_2‚îÉMastery
gameTip_Desc_10160_2‚îÉ<color=#8FDBF8>Mastery</color> is divided into Defense, Hunting, Crafting, Movement, Search, and Weapons.\nYou gain Mastery when you meet the requirements for each of the above, and you level up when you accumulate a certain number of Masteries.\nYou can learn more about the requirements on the <color=#8FDBF8>Character Info Section</color>.
gameTip_Title_10070_3‚îÉLaunch Pad
gameTip_Desc_10070_3‚îÉThe Launch Pad allows you to easily and quickly move to distant areas!\nThey can be crucial in confrontations with enemies, so practice using them strategically!
gameTip_Title_10401_1‚îÉIntroducing Weather Variables
gameTip_Desc_10401_1‚îÉLumia Island's weather is influenced by 1 main and 1 sub weather, with different effects being applied based on the corresponding weather conditions.\nYou can check which weather variables you've been assigned through the cinematic intro after matching or in-game.
gameTip_Title_10402_1‚îÉMain Weather
gameTip_Desc_10402_1‚îÉThe main weather types are Cloudy, Clear, Rainy and Sandstorm.\n\nCloudy: Lumia Island's default weather with no effects applied.\n\nClear: An optimal environment. This weather facilitates food crafting and visibility.\n\nRainy: Perfect for hiding. The number of brushes in the island is increased and hearing range is reduced.\n\nSandstorm: A very restrictive environment. Test subjects gain tenacity and whirlwinds are created.
gameTip_Title_10402_2‚îÉMain Weather - Clear
gameTip_Desc_10402_2‚îÉOverall there are less brushes around the island and your vision range is increased.\nYour recovery from crafted consumables is also increased.\nYou have higher chances to get Rare Materials from collecting consumables.
gameTip_Title_10402_3‚îÉMain Weather - Rainy
gameTip_Desc_10402_3‚îÉOverall there are more brushes around the island.\nYour hearing range decreases and wild animals' attack power is reduced. 
gameTip_Title_10402_4‚îÉMain Weather - Sandstorm
gameTip_Desc_10402_4‚îÉTest subjects that withstand the harsh sandtorm gain Tenacity.\nA whirlwind is created, making targets hit by it untargetable. Targets caught in the whirlwind are able to move over walls after a certain time. 
gameTip_Title_10403_1‚îÉSub Weather
gameTip_Desc_10403_1‚îÉThe sub weather types are Windless, Purple Fog, Strong Winds and Lightning.\n\nWindless: Lumia Island's default weather with no effects applied.\n\nPurple Fog: Mutant spawn rates increase, and all characters deal increased damage to wild animals. \n\nStrong Winds: Perfect for moving faster. Your basic movement speed increases and strong wind zones increase your movement speed in one direction.\n\nLightning: A threatening environment. Lightning strikes occur randomly outdoors, except in restricted areas.
gameTip_Title_10403_2‚îÉSub Weather - Purple Fog
gameTip_Desc_10403_2‚îÉDuring certain phases, Fog Zones are created. These zones have the following effects:\nAll wild animals within the Fog zones are eliminated.\nFog Nests spawn at random locations within the Fog zones.\nApproaching a Fog Nest spawns an aggressive herd of mutant monsters.
gameTip_Title_10403_3‚îÉSub Weather - Strong Winds
gameTip_Desc_10403_3‚îÉYour base movement speed is increased.\nStrong Wind zones are created in specific areas, increasing your movement speed in one direction.
gameTip_Title_10403_4‚îÉSub Weather - Lightning
gameTip_Desc_10403_4‚îÉLightning strikes happen all around the island except in Restricted Areas.\nThe frequency of lightning strikes increases over the course of the experiment.
gameTip_Title_10404_1‚îÉIntroducing Eco Objectives
gameTip_Desc_10404_1‚îÉThere are 5 Eco Objectives and each of them apply different effects. These are: Glowing Flower, White Lily, Healing Spray, Sandy Mushroom and Bomb Fruit.
gameTip_Title_10404_2‚îÉEco Objective - Glowing Flower
gameTip_Desc_10404_2‚îÉThe flower emits a fan-shaped wave in the direction of the attack.\nThe fan-shaped wave travels a certain distance, granting vision and making you visible to your enemy test subjects within range for a certain period of time.
gameTip_Title_10404_3‚îÉEco Objective - White Lily
gameTip_Desc_10404_3‚îÉTest subjects within range are knocked back.\nAirborne test subjects can go over walls.
gameTip_Title_10404_4‚îÉEco Objective - Healing Spray
gameTip_Desc_10404_4‚îÉWhen the objective is under attack it sprays a mist that heals tests subjects within a certain range.\nHealing is applied to both allies and enemies.
gameTip_Title_10404_5‚îÉEco Objective - Sandy Mushroom
gameTip_Desc_10404_5‚îÉThis objective releases cloud of sand, granting invisibility within range for a certain period of time.
gameTip_Title_10404_6‚îÉEco Objective - Bomb Fruit
gameTip_Desc_10404_6‚îÉFruits are released in a fan shape in the direction of the attack, dealing damage.\nFruit damage does not affect allies and is proportional to max hp.\nThe fruits also deal additional damage to Wild Animals.
gameTip_Title_10501_1‚îÉGadget Introduction
gameTip_Desc_10501_1‚îÉGadgets are usable items that provide useful effects during experiments.\nYou can obtain gadgets through various means such as mutant wild animals or air supplies, and each player can only hold one type of gadget.
gameTip_Title_10502_1‚îÉKiosk Remote
gameTip_Desc_10502_1‚îÉHave you ever been unable to use Credits because the area where the Kiosk was located was occupied?\nNow, with the 'Kiosk Remote' gadget obtained from mutant wild animals, you can summon a Mobile Kiosk drone from any location and use your credits.
gameTip_Title_10503_1‚îÉFlare Guns
gameTip_Desc_10503_1‚îÉThese flare guns embody the saying, "A crisis is an opportunity."\nCollect one of the two types of flare guns, Flare Gun B and Flare Gun R, from Epic Air Supply boxes and turn crises into opportunities.
gameTip_Title_10503_2‚îÉFlare Gun B
gameTip_Desc_10503_2‚îÉUsing the Flare Gun B allows you to select a specific point within a wide area to call a Mythic Air Supply box.\nThe location is revealed to all test subjects, but it can turn a crisis into an opportunity as you get a chance to acquire powerful mythic items.
gameTip_Title_10503_3‚îÉFlare Gun R
gameTip_Desc_10503_3‚îÉUse the Flare Gun R to become the spawn point of the air supply.\n(Upon death, the supply box will arrive to the location of your corpse)\nAlthough the location is revealed to all test subjects within a certain range, overcoming this crisis can lead to obtaining powerful mythic items.
gameTip_Title_10504_1‚îÉMobile VLS
gameTip_Desc_10504_1‚îÉThe Mobile VLS provides the same functionality as the standard VLS but is a single-use gadget that enables synchronized gliding with allies.\nWhile you're gliding, the ground becomes visible below a certain altitude, making it excellent for scouting.\nLanding after gliding triggers a brief area-wide stun, making it useful for initiating.\nOnly the Host can control the gliding, with allies relying on the Host for synchronized movement. At a certain altitude, allies can break away from the Host and move freely.
gameTip_Title_10511_1‚îÉGuide Robot - Lumi
gameTip_Desc_10511_1‚îÉLumi is a cute guide robot that roams around Lumia Island, selling special items.\nOnce it accumulates a certain amount of credits, you can steal them from Lumi.
gameTip_Title_10512_1‚îÉGuide Robot - Special Shop
gameTip_Desc_10512_1‚îÉLumi will stop and greet you when you get close to it during the game. Right-click on it to interact with it and purchase special items not available at Kiosks at a low price. Once you buy an item, the guide robot will dash away at high speed. If you don't purchase anything, it will take a short break, temporarily stop selling items and move around slowly.
gameTip_Title_10513_1‚îÉGuide Robot: Credits Charged
gameTip_Desc_10513_1‚îÉSpend Credits at the Kiosks, with the Guide Robot, or using Remote Drones to help the Guide Robot accumulate enough Credits to enter a "Credits Charged" state.\nApproach the Guide Robot in this state and right-click to channel for a short time. The Guide Robot will then enter a 'Alert Mode', shooting energy projectiles at nearby test subjects as it flees.\nAttack the fleeing Guide Robot and deal enough damage to make it drop Credits on the ground. Collecting these Credits will reward you with an amount of Credits proportional to your character's level.
gameTip_Title_10514_1‚îÉGuide Robot - Detailed Explanation
gameTip_Desc_10514_1‚îÉThe Guide Robot's revival function is available until Restricted Areas become active after Day 5. If you lose a teammate during late game, look for the Guide Robot for a turnaround.\nYou can always check the Guide Robot's location on the Expanded Map/Minimap. Since the Guide Robot dislikes Restricted Areas, it moves at five times the normal speed within them.
gameTip_Title_10521_1‚îÉWhat are Cubes?
gameTip_Desc_10521_1‚îÉCubes are objectives that grant special effects to the character who collects them, depending on their type.\nThe type of cube is determined one minute before it spawns, and you can obtain it by directly touching the cube once it spawns.
gameTip_Title_10522_1‚îÉSanguine Cube
gameTip_Desc_10522_1‚îÉThe red Sanguine Cube grants the character who collects it a powerful ability to deal continuous damage over time.
gameTip_Title_10523_1‚îÉGalaxy Cube
gameTip_Desc_10523_1‚îÉThe purple Galaxy Cube grants the character who collects it reduced cooldowns, an increased max cooldown reduction and a skill amp increase, as well as partial recovery of lost SP periodically and additional damage based on Skill Amplification.
gameTip_Title_10524_1‚îÉProsperity Cube
gameTip_Desc_10524_1‚îÉThe golden Prosperity Cube grants the character who collects it Credits proportional to their level shortly after.
gameTip_Title_10525_1‚îÉEssence Cube
gameTip_Desc_10525_1‚îÉThe green Essence Cube grants the character who collects it the ability to recover health proportional to their max HP for a short time when their HP drops below a certain amount and they take damage.
gameTip_Title_10526_1‚îÉZephyr Cube
gameTip_Desc_10526_1‚îÉThe sky-blue Zephyr Cube grants the character who acquires it a burst of movement speed for a short time after collecting it, significantly increasing their movement speed, out-of-combat movement speed and attack speed for the duration of the buff. During combat, movement speed is increased.
gameTip_Title_10621_1‚îÉWhat is a Secret Lab?
gameTip_Desc_10621_1‚îÉA Secret Lab is a hidden location within Lumia Island where you can obtain special rewards without other teams getting on your way.\nYou can only enter the lab using the Mobile Loop that is activated with a Loop Key Card and you can return to Lumia Island using the Loop inside the lab.
gameTip_Title_10622_1‚îÉLoop Card Keys
gameTip_Desc_10622_1‚îÉThe Loop Key Cards grant access to the Secret Lab. These can be obtained from Wild Animals.
gameTip_Title_10623_1‚îÉMobile Loop
gameTip_Desc_10623_1‚îÉUse the Loop Key Card to activate a portable loop that grants access to a Secret Lab.\n\nThe portable loop can only be accessed at designated times during the game, and you can only access one Secret Lab per game.\n\nIf all labs are full, you've already visited a lab, or the entry time has passed, the loop turns red and deactivates.
gameTip_Title_10624_1‚îÉAbout the Secret Lab
gameTip_Desc_10624_1‚îÉOnce you arrive at a Secret Lab, activate the door console to earn credits and open the door. \n\nExplore the entire lab, kill drones, open boxes, and more. When the timer at the top ends, the lab becomes a Restricted Area. \nMake sure to exit the lab using the loop before you run out of time.
gameTip_Title_10624_2‚îÉBlue Lab
gameTip_Desc_10624_2‚îÉAs you kill the lab‚Äôs drones and push forward, you‚Äôll eventually come face-to-face with a powerful hidden presence.\nDefeat it to claim special rewards!
gameTip_Title_10801_1‚îÉIntroducing Rift
gameTip_Desc_10801_1‚îÉThe Rift is a phenomenon caused by abnormal VF energy. It is an objective that teleports nearby test subjects to another space. The type of Rift spawned varies depending on the time of appearance and the number of remaining teams.
gameTip_Title_10802_1‚îÉDimensional Rift
gameTip_Desc_10802_1‚îÉThis Rift spawns mainly during the day. It teleports any test subjects who enter it to the Combat Lab. A Dimensional Rift can be used by only one team.
gameTip_Title_10802_2‚îÉCombat Lab
gameTip_Desc_10802_2‚îÉTest subjects who enter through the Dimensional Rift are teleported to the Combat Lab, where all dead allies are revived. Only two teams can enter the Dimensional Rift and the team that achieves victory earns a powerful reward. Wiped teams in a Combat Lab accesible through a regular Dimensional Rift are revived. However, teams wiped out in a Combat Lab entered through an Enhanced Dimensional Rift (available from Day 3) are eliminated from the experiment.
gameTip_Title_10802_3‚îÉDimensional Rift - Enhanced
gameTip_Desc_10802_3‚îÉFrom Day 3 onward, the Dimensional Rift and Combat Lab are affected by stronger VF energy, altering their appearance and combat mechanics. If your team is wiped out in the Combat Lab during this phase, you are immediately eliminated from the game. Be fully prepared before stepping into this fight for survival.
gameTip_Title_10803_1‚îÉWarp
gameTip_Desc_10803_1‚îÉTest subjects who remain on the Warp for a set period are sucked in and launched into the sky from the Warp exit at the entrance of the Research Center in the Forest. If you want to travel to a specific location, use the VLS instead. If teammates remain on the same Warp, they can hold hands and move together, just like when using the VLS.
gameTip_Title_10300_1‚îÉIntroducing Area Gimmicks
gameTip_Desc_10300_1‚îÉLumia Island features unique area gimmicks in the form of objectives that match each area's concept and offer new strategic effects.
gameTip_Title_10301_1‚îÉPolice Station - Evidence Boxes
gameTip_Title_10302_1‚îÉSchool - Grandfather Clock
gameTip_Desc_10302_1‚îÉGo to School to find a large Grandfather Clock that turns back time.\nOperate it to recover some of your detonation timer and your skill cooldowns (basic, tactical, and weapon).
gameTip_Title_10303_1‚îÉHospital - Medical Support Network
gameTip_Desc_10303_1‚îÉThe Hospital is an area with an enhanced Medical System that aids in test subjects' survival.\nUse the Hospital Kiosk to revive allies at a discounted price.\nKill Hospital Bears to collect First Aid Kits which grant much higher recovery than any common food items.
gameTip_Title_10304_1‚îÉBarge - Cranes & Barrels
gameTip_Desc_10304_1‚îÉUse the dynamic environment of the Barge to your advantage to disrupt enemies.\nA crane in the area periodically rotates and drops barrels onto the ground.\nStriking a dropped barrel rolls it in the direction of the attack. On impact, the rolling barrel creates a zone that continuously slows test subjects.
gameTip_Title_10305_1‚îÉApple Tree
gameTip_Desc_10305_1‚îÉGet additional supplies from the Apple Tree!\nAttack the tree to get Apples ready for consumption.\nYou can even get Golden Apples, which offer higher hp regen, at a lower chance.
gameTip_Title_10050_3‚îÉBox Group Display
gameTip_Desc_10050_3‚îÉDisplays in blue the boxes closest to the starting point for each area.\nBy opening all the boxes in the same group, you can get at least all the target items for that area.\nThis is important information when deciding your Early-game route, so check the boxes with the <color=#8FDBF8>box group indicator</color> first!

Tutorial/Desc/1‚îÉWelcome to Lumia Island.\n\nJackie will teach you the basics of item searching in order to survive on this island.\n\nTo think you'll learn from a Serial Killer! You're in good hands, right?
Tutorial/Desc/2‚îÉLearn about skills and fighting with the Axe weapon. The axe is good to learn with because it's stronger than other weapons in the beginning of the match.\n\nFind enemies and kill them. The axe will become weaker as the game progresses.
Tutorial/Desc/3‚îÉYou can become stronger by killing wild animals.\n\nYou'll play as Aya. She might seem careless, but she's still a police officer handling a gun.
Tutorial/Desc/4‚îÉTry exploring Lumia Island a bit more.\n\nJackie is an intelligent murderer. Try efficiently finding materials so you can craft several targeted items at the same time.
Tutorial/Desc/5‚îÉYour weapon and defense masteries increase as you fight.\n\nMoving around increases your movement mastery and making equipment increases your crafting mastery.\n\nAya might seem like a weak rookie with a puny sniper rifle,\nbut you'll change your mind once she lands a shot on you after increasing her sniper rifle mastery.

GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/PREFACE‚îÉPreface
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/EternalReturn‚îÉEternal Return
GuideBook/Page/Content/EternalReturn/1‚îÉEternal Return is a <color=white>Battle Royale</color> game, where test subjects compete to become the final survivor.
GuideBook/Page/Content/EternalReturn/2‚îÉThis guide will take you through the most basic tips you need to know to master the game from start to end.
GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/GROWTH‚îÉOpening
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/EpicReady‚îÉCraft
GuideBook/Page/Content/EpicReady/1‚îÉ<color=white>(1/3)</color>\n\nUpon arrival, your first assignment is to craft <color=yellow>starting target items</color> in your Saved Plan.\n\nFrom your spawn point, you will notice <color=yellow>boxes</color> that are highlighted in blue. Boxes hold items that are distinct to the area you are in.\n\nLook out for the items marked with a <color=yellow>yellow triangle</color>. These are the items you need to craft the equipment in your Saved Plan.
GuideBook/Page/Content/EpicReadyTwo/1‚îÉ<color=white>(2/3)</color>\n\nYou can also acquire items by <color=white>hunting wild animals, collecting natural resources, and calling remote drones</color>.\n\nPress Z to combine these materials until you craft all the <color=white>target items in your Saved Plan</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/EpicReadyThree/1‚îÉ<color=white>(3/3)</color>\n\nYou can check your starting target items on the <color=white>upper right corner</color>. Once you've finished crafting your your starting target items, they will automatically be updated into your late game equipments.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TeamUp‚îÉTeam Up
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamUp/1‚îÉOnce you've crafted all the target equipment in your Saved Plan, you can use the <color=yellow>Hyperloop</color> to join your teammates.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamUp/2‚îÉClick on your <color=white>teammate's portrait</color> on the upper right corner of the Hyperloop UI to teleport to their location.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamUp/3‚îÉDuring early game, up until <color=yellow>Night 2</color>, you and your teammates can revive after a brief waiting time.\nYou can select the location where you'd like to revive, including your teammate's location by clicking on their <color=white>portrait</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/Hunt‚îÉHunt
GuideBook/Page/Content/Hunt/1‚îÉStart hunting <color=yellow>wild animals</color> with your teammates.\nUse the security console in each area to track their locations, which will be displayed on your <color=white>Minimap</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/Hunt/2‚îÉHunt wild animals to get <color=yellow>Credits</color> and gain <color=yellow>Mastery</color>. \n<color=yellow>Credits</color> are a valuable resource in Lumia Island that can be used to buy special materials or revive your teammates.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/FindFood‚îÉFood
GuideBook/Page/Content/FindFood/1‚îÉYou can recover HP by consuming <color=yellow>Food</color>.\nThis effect will <color=white>persist even in battle</color>, so be sure to stack them in your inventory.
GuideBook/Page/Content/FindFood/2‚îÉYou can obtain raw ingredients from <color=white>wild animals, boxes, or by collecting them in the wild</color>.\nWhile you can consume them to recover HP, they are not very effective.\n\nFind a <color=yellow>Campfire</color> to cook these raw ingredients into more effective food.\n\nYou can also find moderately effective food in <color=yellow>Food Air Supply Boxes</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TreeOfLife‚îÉTree of Life / Meteorite
GuideBook/Page/Content/TreeOfLife/1‚îÉ<color=yellow>On Day 2</color>, two special meterials will spawn on Lumia Island: <color=yellow>Tree of Life</color> and <color=yellow>Meteorite</color>.\n\n<color=yellow>Tree of Life</color> has a fixed spawn point, while <color=yellow>Meteorite</color> collisions will happen at random locations on the island each game.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TreeOfLife/2‚îÉ<color=yellow>Special Materials</color> are <color=white>highly valued rare resources</color> in Lumia Island. This means that you must be ready to <color=white>engage in combat</color> with other teams to acquire these materials.\n\nTry not to lose!
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/UpgradeItems‚îÉEquipment Upgrade
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItems/1‚îÉYou need <color=yellow>Special Materials</color> to craft <color=yellow>Legendary items</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItems/2‚îÉEach teammate may need different <color=yellow>special materials</color>, so be sure to distribute them strategically.
GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/EXPLORATION‚îÉMiddlegame
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/UpgradeItemsTwo‚îÉSpecial Materials
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItemsTwo/1‚îÉ<color=white>(1/2)</color>\n\nYou can obtain the special materials needed to craft your late-game equipment from the island's main objectives, like <color=white>Tree of Life, Meteorite, Alpha/Omega and Air Supplies</color>
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItemsTwo/2‚îÉYou can also purchase them from <color=yellow>Kiosks</color> that you can find in different areas across the island.
GuideBook/Page/Content/UpgradeItemsThree/1‚îÉ<color=white>(2/2)</color>\n\n<color=yellow>Main objectives</color> are usually displayed on your <color=white>Minimap</color>. \nYou can also check their spawn times on the Scoreboard by pressing the <color=white>Tab Key</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/RestrictedArea‚îÉRestricted Areas
GuideBook/Page/Content/RestrictedArea/1‚îÉEach Day and Night, a random area on the island will become a <color=red>Restricted Area</color>, marked in <color=red>red</color> on your Expanded Map. Upcoming Restricted Areas are marked in <color=yellow>orange</color> before they're designated as such.
GuideBook/Page/Content/RestrictedArea/2‚îÉEvery second spent at a Restricted Area will consume your <color=red>restriction timer</color>. Once you run out of time, you are instantly <color=red>eliminated</color>.
GuideBook/Page/Content/RestrictedArea/3‚îÉHowever, don‚Äôt hesitate to use your restriction timer during a <color=white>tough situation</color>. Sometimes, <color=white>escaping into a Restricted Area</color> can be the smart choice for survival. After all, you can restore your timer by <color=white>eliminating enemy characters</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/Transport‚îÉTransportation
GuideBook/Page/Content/Transport/1‚îÉThere are various means of transportation on Lumia Island.\n\n<color=yellow>Hyperloops</color> take you to any area you choose or directly to your teammate‚Äôs location.\n<color=yellow>White Lily</color> lets you jump a short distance across walls.\n<color=yellow>Launch Pads</color> help you jump <color=white>longer distances</color> over obstacles.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/Vision‚îÉSecure Vision
GuideBook/Page/Content/Vision/1‚îÉAlways keep an eye out! <color=yellow>Vision</color> helps you make smarter battle decisions, and you and your teammates should always be aware of your surroundings.\n\n<color=yellow>Security Consoles</color> reveal the surrounding area when activated.\n<color=yellow>Glowing Flowers</color> detonate to briefly grant vision in the opposite direction.\n<color=yellow>Telephoto Cameras and Drones</color> can be installed from your inventory to provide vision of their surroundings, so be sure to install them in strategic locations.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TeamFight‚îÉTeam Fight
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamFight/1‚îÉUsing your character's <color=white>Skills, Tactical Skills and Weapon Skills</color> efficiently is the key to winning team fights.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamFight/2‚îÉ<color=white>Starting on Day 2</color>, if <color=yellow>you and your teammates</color> are all downed or eliminated, your experiment will be <color=red>terminated</color>.\nIf the enemy team is <color=red>too strong</color>, always consider <color=white>retreating</color> and waiting for a better opportunity.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TeamFight/3‚îÉWhen a character's HP reaches zero, they enter a <color=yellow>Downed State</color>.\nRight-click on a <color=white>downed ally</color> to revive them.\nEliminate a <color=white>downed enemy</color> to recover some HP and loot their Credits, Consumables, and Food.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/Resurrection‚îÉRevival
GuideBook/Page/Content/Resurrection/1‚îÉStarting from <color=yellow>Day 3</color>, you can use <color=white>Credits</color> at a <color=yellow>Kiosk</color> to revive eliminated allies.\nIf you don‚Äôt have enough Credits, the revived test subject's Credits will be used automatically.
GuideBook/Page/Content/Resurrection/2‚îÉStarting from <color=yellow>Day 5</color>, <color=white>you can no longer revive allies at the Kiosk.</color>
GuideBook/MainTheme/Title/FINALBATTLE‚îÉEndgame
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/BattleReady‚îÉPreparation
GuideBook/Page/Content/BattleReady/1‚îÉ<color=yellow>Dr. Wickeline</color> spawns once the end of the match is approaching.\n<color=white>Eliminating</color> her grants you and your teammates a <color=yellow>powerful buff</color>. She also drops <color=yellow>VF Blood Sample</color>, a special material you can use to craft Mythic items.
GuideBook/Page/Content/BattleReady/2‚îÉYou can craft Mythic items using <color=yellow>VF Blood Sample</color> or you can collect them from <color=yellow>Mythic Air Supplies</color>.\nYou can also purchase a <color=yellow>VF Blood Sample</color> from a <color=yellow>Kiosk</color> using your Credits.
GuideBook/Page/Content/BattleReady/3‚îÉPrepare for the endgame by stocking up on <color=white>food and cameras.</color>\nNote: You can also purchase these items through the <color=yellow>V Pad</color><color=white> (Hotkey V)</color>.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/TemporarySafeZone‚îÉTemporary Safe Zones
GuideBook/Page/Content/TemporarySafeZone/1‚îÉ<color=yellow>Temporary Safe Zones</color> serve as battlegrounds leading up to the final showdown.\nWhen only three safe areas remain, <color=yellow>the two non-adjacent areas</color> will become temporary safe zones.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TemporarySafeZone/2‚îÉOnly <color=yellow>one team</color> can survive in each temporary safe zone.\nChoose the <color=white>most advantageous battlefield</color> for your team and move accordingly.
GuideBook/Page/Content/TemporarySafeZone/3‚îÉAll <color=yellow>dead or downed teammates</color> of the team that survive the temporary safe zone will be brought back into the experiment.
GuideBook/SubTheme/Title/LastSafeZone‚îÉFinal Safe Zone
GuideBook/Page/Content/LastSafeZone/1‚îÉOnly the <color=yellow>final battle</color> remains to determine the ultimate winner.\nThe <color=yellow>Chronosphere</color> is designated in the <color=yellow>Final Safe Zone</color>in the area bridging the two temporary safe zones.\nHurry over to the <color=white>Final Safe Zone</color> forming around the Chronosphere!
GuideBook/Page/Content/LastSafeZone/2‚îÉSecure your final victory and claim everything.\n<color=white>Let there be glory to the pawns who reached the end of despair!</color>

Tutorial/Title/Ch1/BasicControls/Title‚îÉControls Guide
Tutorial/Title/Ch1/BasicControls/Desc‚îÉTry controlling your character using the mouse and keyboard!\n\nYou can <color=#8FDBF8>move</color>, <color=#8FDBF8>select</color>, or <color=#8FDBF8>attack</color> a target with <color=#FFCE2B>Right-click</color>, and use your character's <color=#8FDBF8>Normal Skills</color> with <color=#FFCE2B>Q, W, and E</color>.\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>D</color> for the <color=#8FDBF8>Weapon Skill</color> corresponding to your character's weapon type, and <color=#FFCE2B>F</color> for the <color=#8FDBF8>Tactical Skill</color> selected before the experiment began.\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>R</color> to unleash your character's finishing move, their <color=#8FDBF8>Ultimate</color>, and deal a powerful blow to your enemies!
Tutorial/Img/Ch2/ItemFarming/Title‚îÉItem Farming
Tutorial/Img/Ch2/ItemFarming/Desc‚îÉAre you having trouble distinguishing the boxes located on Lumia Island?\nLook for the <color=#8FDBF8>box</color> <color=#8FDBF8>icon</color> to find boxes you haven't checked yet!\n\nNot sure which item to take after opening a box?\nPay attention to items with a <color=#8FDBF8>yellow mark</color>. These are items you must obtain.\n \nDon't know which items you need to collect in your current area?\nDon't worry! The <color=#8FDBF8>Crafting Guide on the right side of the screen</color> shows you the items you can obtain!\n\nSearch only your required materials, so you can craft your targeted equipment!!
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_1/Title‚îÉSaved Plans
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_1/Desc_1‚îÉDon't memorize! Use Saved Plans!\n\nA Saved Plan is essentially your <color=#8FDBF8>farming route</color>. By pre-registering the areas you need to visit and your targeted equipment in a Saved Plan, you can quickly obtain materials without getting lost!\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>M</color> to open the <color=#8FDBF8>Expanded Map</color> and check your Saved Plan's route.\n\nIf you're unsure what to do, try crafting items quickly by following your Saved Plan!
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_2/Title‚îÉCollecting Materials
Tutorial/Img/Ch3/Route_2/Desc‚îÉI know you can't see flowers, stones, or branches in your Saved Plan.\nThat's because these items cannot be obtained from boxes!\n\nYou can collect <color=#8FDBF8>Flowers</color>, <color=#8FDBF8>Stones</color>, and <color=#8FDBF8>Branches</color> from the ground around Lumia Island.\n\nIf you need these items to craft equipment, keep a close eye on your surroundings and collect them!
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/GamePhase/Title‚îÉGame Phases
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/GamePhase/Desc‚îÉGame phases change according to the time on Lumia Island, bringing gradual changes to the it!\n\nThis includes Day/Night cycles that affect your <color=#8FDBF8>Vision</color> and types of <color=#8FDBF8>Wild Animals</color>, as well as changes to the experiment such as designating restricted areas, spawning boss monsters, and team revival rules.\n\nPaying attention to these changes as the game phase progresses will bring you closer to becoming the final survivor!
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/ItemCalling/Title‚îÉRequesting Items
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/ItemCalling/Desc_1‚îÉIf the material you want isn't in a box, try using a Remote Drone!\n\nYou can have the item you want delivered by a Remote Drone at any time and place by right-clicking it in your targeted item's list!\n\nHowever, keep in mind that you need <color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color> to use a Remote Drone!
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/ItemCalling/Desc_2‚îÉ<color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color>, which serve as currency on Lumia Island, can be obtained through various actions.\n\n- First: They are distributed at <color=#8FDBF8>regular intervals</color>.\n- Second: You can get them by killing a <color=#8FDBF8>test subject</color>.\n- Third: You can get them by hunting <color=#8FDBF8>wild animals</color>.
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/NextRoute/Title‚îÉLate-game Items
Tutorial/Img/Ch4/NextRoute/Desc‚îÉDon't be surprised if a new item list suddenly appears after you've crafted all your targeted items!\n\nYour items list automatically changes to the <color=#8FDBF8>late-game items</color> registered in your Saved Plan.\n\nFarming and crafting the required materials for these newly registered late-game items will bring you closer to becoming the final survivor!
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/SecurityConsole/Title‚îÉSecurity Console
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/SecurityConsole/Desc‚îÉOperate Security Consoles around the island to gain the most crucial information, vision, and locate your opponents, obtaining a strategic advantage!\n\nOperating a Security Console activates the CCTVs in that area, allowing you to secure vision around the cameras. You also earn a small amount of Credits for operating a Security Console not currently occupied by allies.\n\nConversely, if a CCTV's red light is on and moving toward you, it means someone is watching! Run away immediately!
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/FoodItem/Title‚îÉFood
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/FoodItem/Desc_1‚îÉLow on HP?\n\nTake the food items you've obtained from hunting Wild Animals or from boxes and head to a <color=#8FDBF8>Campfire</color>!\n\nYou can craft high-<color=#8FDBF8>Rarity</color> food at a Campfire using food items.
Tutorial/Img/Ch6/FoodItem/Desc_2‚îÉDid you not find any food items?\n\nDon't worry!\nOn Lumia Island, you can obtain food by collecting <color=#8FDBF8>Potatoes</color>, <color=#8FDBF8>Edible Mushrooms</color> or <color=#8FDBF8>Fishing</color>. Occasionally, you can even find rare food items.\n\nDon't worry if there are no potatoes, edible mushrooms, or fishing spots nearby! You can also obtain food through <color=#8FDBF8>Air Supply Boxes</color>!
Tutorial/Img/Ch7/SpecialObject/Title‚îÉSpecial Objectives
Tutorial/Img/Ch7/SpecialObject/Desc_1‚îÉSpecial objectives spawn all over Lumia Island.\n\nFrom items that can be collected, such as <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> and <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>, to <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>, and <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Samples</color>!\n\nUse these special materials to craft <color=#FFCE2B>powerful items</color>!
Tutorial/Img/Ch7/SpecialObject/Desc_2‚îÉDoes your targeted item require materials other than <color=#D05EFF>Tree of Life</color> or <color=#D05EFF>Meteorite</color>?\n\nThen check the minimap. If you see a boss monster, that's your chance to obtain special materials!\n\nKill boss monsters to obtain <color=#D05EFF>Mythril</color>, <color=#D05EFF>Force Core</color>, and <color=#D05EFF>VF Blood Samples</color>!
Tutorial/Img/Ch9/SpecialItem/Title‚îÉObtaining Special Materials
Tutorial/Img/Ch9/SpecialItem/Desc_1‚îÉDid you know there are different ways to obtain special materials?\n\nIn addition to collecting Tree of Life and Meteorites or killing specific boss monsters, you can obtain them by <color=#8FDBF8>killing Mutant Wild Animals</color> or <color=#8FDBF8>opening Epic Air Supply Boxes (Purple Boxes)</color>.
Tutorial/Img/Ch9/SpecialItem/Desc_2‚îÉIf you have enough <color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color>, you can also order them through the <color=#8FDBF8>Kiosks</color> located around Lumia Island.\n\nThe amount of <color=#8FDBF8>Credits</color> required varies for each special material. If you fail to get one, find a <color=#8FDBF8>Kiosk</color> and <color=#FFCE2B>order</color> the special material you need!
Tutorial/Img/Ch10/RestrictedArea/Title‚îÉRestricted Areas
Tutorial/Img/Ch10/RestrictedArea/Desc‚îÉAre you currently in a restricted area <color=#8FDBF8>marked in red on the minimap</color>? If so, get out quickly! Otherwise, the <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> will activate and kill you.\n\nWhen you are in a restricted area, the <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> activates along with an <color=#8FDBF8>ominous effect</color>, and if the <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> reaches 0, your character will immediately <color=#8FDBF8>die</color>.\n\nYou can recover time on your detonation timer by killing other test subjects, but it's always to simply stay out of restricted areas.\n\n"The test subject will explode shortly!‚Äù
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/NextRestrictedArea/Title‚îÉRestricted Area Designation
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/NextRestrictedArea/Desc‚îÉIf you see <color=#8FDBF8>orange</color> in your current area, <color=#8FDBF8>be careful</color>!\nThis means it will <color=#8FDBF8>become a Restricted Area</color> when the next Game Phase begins.\n\nPress <color=#FFCE2B>M</color> right now to open the <color=#8FDBF8>Expanded Map</color> and check your current area.\nIf it's marked in <color=#8FDBF8>orange</color>, it's an upcoming Restricted Area.\n\nThis means it's not dangerous right now, but it will <color=#8FDBF8>become a Restricted Area when the Game Phase changes</color>. Evacuate to a safe area immediately!
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/VLS/Title‚îÉVLS
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/VLS/Desc_1‚îÉThe VLS or vertical launching device does not allow long-distance travel but enables simultaneous movement with teammates through gliding.\n\nIt is also excellent for reconnaissance, as you can see the ground from below a certain altitude while gliding.\n\nOn <color=#8FDBF8>Day 3</color>, Hyperloops in all areas except restricted areas are replaced with vertical launching devices.
Tutorial/Img/Ch11/VLS/Desc_2‚îÉWhen one teammate activates the VLS, that teammate becomes the Host, and other teammates can hop on the device as well.\n\nOnly the Host can control the glide, and other teammates will glide along as companions to the Host. Upon landing, it <color=#8FDBF8>stuns</color> test subjects within the area for a moment, making it useful for initiation.\n\nFrom a certain altitude, you can break free from the current Host teammate and move freely.
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZone/Title‚îÉTemporary Safe Zones
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZone/Desc_1‚îÉWhen the last remaining safe zone is designated as a restricted area, if <color=#8FDBF8>3 or more teams have survived</color>, two temporary safe zones will be created.\n\nWhen a temporary safe zone is <color=#8FDBF8>activated</color>, the area becomes <color=#8FDBF8>isolated</color> from all others. During this time, you are not able to enter or escape.\n\nIf you move out of the temporary safe zone, your <color=#8FDBF8>Detonation Timer</color> is <color=#8FDBF8>triggered</color>. Move to an active Temporary Safe Zone quickly to preserve your <color=#8FDBF8>Detonation Timer</color> and prepare for combat!
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZone/Desc_2‚îÉWhen a temporary safe zone is <color=#8FDBF8>activated</color>, that area is <color=#8FDBF8>isolated</color> from other areas, so be careful.\n\nWhile isolated, you cannot enter that area or escape to others.\n\nMove quickly to an activated temporary safe zone to freeze your <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> and prepare for combat!
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZoneInfo/Title‚îÉTemporary Safe Zones
Tutorial/Img/Ch12/TemporarySafeZoneInfo/Desc‚îÉIs your Detonation Timer running out even though you're in a Temporary Safe Zone?\n\nIf <color=#8FDBF8>only your team is located</color> within a Temporary Safe Zone, the Detonation Timer will not activate. However, if <color=#8FDBF8>2 or more teams are located</color> in one Temporary Safe Zone, or if you <color=#8FDBF8>go outside</color> the Temporary Safe Zone, the Detonation Timer will get triggered.\n\nIt's simple: just make sure only your team is in the Temporary Safe Zone! Chase away the enemies and survive!
Tutorial/Img/Ch13/ChronoSphere/Title‚îÉChronosphere
Tutorial/Img/Ch13/ChronoSphere/Desc‚îÉThe final stage to determine the winner between the last two surviving teams on Lumia Island is the <color=#8FDBF8>Final Safe Zone</color>.\n\nThe Final Safe Zone is created in a <color=#8FDBF8>circle around the Chronosphere</color> spawned in the last restricted area. Your <color=#8FDBF8>detonation timer</color> will get triggered in all areas except the safe zone.\n\nThe safe zone will gradually begin to shrink after a certain period, and eventually, all safe zones will disappear, causing your detonation timer to get triggered in all areas. If you don't have much time left on your timer, don't avoid combat with the enemy!
